title: The Loser Stock Your Brain Won't Sell Costs $47K
hook: You're 1.7 times more likely to sell a winning stock than a losing one — and behavioral economists call that the most expensive habit in investing.
angle: The Disposition Effect is a documented behavioral finance bias that costs retail investors thousands — explained through the specific stats that make you immediately recognize your own portfolio behavior.
audience: Anyone who owns stocks, ETFs, or a brokerage account and has ever watched a losing position and told themselves to wait for it to 'come back'
key_numbers: 1.7x more likely to sell winners than losers
1.5% annual return drag for retail investors
2x — losses feel twice as painful as gains feel good
